description_long: This is a rectangular wooden picnic table with two attached benches running parallel to the longer sides of the tabletop. The tabletop is composed of evenly spaced wooden planks, and the benches are supported by sturdy wooden slats connected to the main frame. The table features a simple, functional design with visible cross-bracing beneath the tabletop for added stability. The wood appears to be unfinished or lightly treated, giving it a natural, light tan color. The benches are integrated into the structure, making the entire unit a single, cohesive piece. There are no visible decorative elements or umbrella holes, emphasizing practicality and durability for outdoor use.
description: A light tan wooden picnic table with two integrated benches and a simple, sturdy frame. The design is practical and functional, suitable for outdoor settings.
description_view_0: The front view shows the tabletop surface with two benches on either side and visible cross-bracing beneath.
description_view_1: The side view highlights the thickness of the benches and tabletop, as well as the supporting slats and frame structure.
description_view_2: The rear view mirrors the front, showing the tabletop and benches with the same cross-bracing details.
description_view_3: The opposite side view again emphasizes the bench and tabletop thickness, with the support structure clearly visible.
attribute_tabletop_support_structure: The tabletop is supported by a set of sturdy wooden legs and crossbeams, with additional diagonal braces for stability connecting the benches and table frame.
